title: Towards derived Satake equivalence for symmetric varieties
speaker: Tsao-Hsien Chen
affiliation: University of Minnesota; Member, School of Mathematics
abstract: In an ongoing project of D. Ben-Zvi, Y. Sakellaridis and A. Venkatesh, the authors propose a conjectural generalization of the derived Satake equivalence for complex reductive groups to spherical varieties. I will describe a program aimed at establishing their conjecture in the case of symmetric varieties (an important class of spherical varieties). A key ingredient is the relation between the derived Satake equivalence for symmetric varieties and the geometric Langlands for real reductive groups. This is joint work with David Nadler.
tags: Workshop on Representation Theory and Geometry

proof_stages.label: Complex-group derived Satake as geometric Langlands on P1
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls ordinary and derived geometric Satake for the affine Grassmannian and identifies it with a beta/geometric-Langlands description of sheaves on Bun_G(P1) and coherent sheaves on derived G-check local systems on S2.

proof_stages.label: Symmetric-variety Satake category and BSV spectral conjecture
proof_stages.summary: For a symmetric variety X=G/K, the speaker defines the derived Satake category Shv(LX/L^+G), reviews its orbit parametrization and Gaitsgory-Nadler Tannakian subcategory, and states the Ben-Zvi-Sakellaridis-Venkatesh conjectural description by coherent sheaves on V_X^check/G_X^check.

proof_stages.label: Symmetric pairs as real forms and real Bun_G(P1)
proof_stages.summary: A symmetric pair is related to a compatible real form G_R and compact real form, and this real form is used to define the real moduli stack of G-bundles on the split real projective line.

proof_stages.label: Real uniformization and affine Matsuki equivalence
proof_stages.summary: The speaker states and outlines the joint theorem with Nadler that real Bun_G(P1) is uniformized by the affine Grassmannian modulo the real polynomial loop group and that sheaves on it are equivalent to the symmetric derived Satake category via a correspondence proved using a real-analytic flow.

proof_stages.label: Main conjectural real-geometric-Langlands diagram
proof_stages.summary: Combining the constructible equivalences with conjectural spectral identifications gives a diagram asserting that derived Satake for symmetric varieties is equivalent to geometric Langlands for the associated real group over P1, with the Nadler dual group matching the Gaitsgory-Nadler dual group.

proof_stages.label: Quaternionic symplectic computation and predicted spectral stack
proof_stages.summary: As an application, the GL_{2n}/Sp_n symmetric variety is related to GL_n over the quaternions, leading to a computation of the relevant Ext algebra as Sym(gl_n[-4]) and to the prediction that the spectral side is gl_n[-3]/GL_n, equivalently GL_n-local systems on S4.

proof_stages.label: Topological proof strategy, variants, and remaining obstructions
proof_stages.summary: The final part explains why topological full-faithfulness methods from modular derived Satake may extend to real affine Grassmannians, discusses the discrepancy with BSV Hamiltonian-dual/non-tempered predictions, and treats questions about other real forms, extensions of dual groups, Kostant slices, and perverse t-structures.

examples.name: Orthogonal symmetric pair
examples.note: The pair GL_n/O_n is used as a concrete symmetric pair whose associated real form is GL_n(R), illustrating the symmetric-pair/real-group dictionary and the Gaitsgory-Nadler dual group discussion.

examples.name: Finite-dimensional Matsuki correspondence and the SL2 picture
examples.note: The classical Matsuki correspondence, especially the SL2 flag-manifold picture with real and complex-orbit stratifications, illustrates the flow argument used in the affine-Grassmannian equivalence.
examples.engagement_reason: the SL2 Matsuki picture is described concretely via hemispheres, S1, poles, complementary orbits, and their orbit correspondence

examples.name: SO(2,R) and PGO(2,R) extension issue
examples.note: These small real groups illustrate why the real Satake side may require a nontrivial Z/2-extension of the dual group to get the correct answer.
